payload.text: 构建一个根据资料内容自动出题的APP，要求一次出30题，有10个选择、10个判断、7个填空和3个简答题

**Formulating the Quiz Structure**

I've been solidifying the question breakdown for the React app. I'm aiming for a total of 30 questions, split into distinct formats. The plan now is to have 10 multiple-choice, 10 true/false, 7 fill-in-the-blank, and 3 short-answer questions. I think this balance offers a good range of difficulty and cognitive engagement. Next up, I will start thinking about the UI framework.


**Developing the React App**

I'm deep into the React app development now. Tailwind CSS is the framework for world-class visuals, with the Gemini API driving the question generation. I've got the project structure mapped out: `metadata.json`, `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `types.ts`, `services/geminiService.ts`, `components/QuizGenerator.tsx`, `components/QuizDisplay.tsx`, and `components/ProgressBar.tsx`. Question formats are sorted out.  I'm using `gemini-3-flash-preview` for speed.  Next step: implementation!


**Refining the Question Generation**

My focus has shifted to the precise structure of the quiz questions. I'm leveraging `GoogleGenAI` and a carefully crafted JSON schema for the Gemini API. The goal is to generate exactly 30 questions with the specified breakdown: 10 multiple-choice, 10 true/false, 7 fill-in-the-blank, and 3 short-answer questions. I've designed a detailed system prompt and schema for this purpose.  Specifically, the system prompt ensures the desired question format.


**Crafting the User Experience**

I'm now zeroing in on the user interface details for this quiz app. I'm focusing on creating visually appealing question cards and a smooth, modern aesthetic using Tailwind CSS – think gradients, shadows, and clean typography.  I'm also exploring CSS transitions and Tailwind animations to enhance the premium feel. The plan is to include a progress bar during quiz generation for a better user experience. Chinese language support is also essential.


**Finalizing Question Types**

I'm making a final push on question formats. The fill-in-the-blanks will have a `question`, `answer`, and `explanation` property. Short answers will feature `question`, a `sampleAnswer`, and `keyPoints`. I'm confident this comprehensive setup will deliver informative and engaging question types.

entries.description: Application metadata including name and description.
Primary entry point for the browser, including Tailwind CSS CDN.
Global TypeScript types and interfaces for the quiz application.
Service for interacting with the Gemini API to generate quiz content.
Component to display the generated quiz questions.
Main application component managing global state and layout.

payload.text: 输入的学习资料修改成PDF、EXcel、Word等附件形式的，且可以在线答题，给出得分

**Enhancing Quiz Functionality**

I've been focusing on enhancing the "Smart Quiz Crafter" app. The main task now is to enable file uploads for PDF, Excel, and Word documents, moving beyond simple text input. I'm also working on creating an interactive quiz environment within the app where users can answer the questions directly. This will greatly improve the user experience.


**Developing Scoring Mechanism**

I've made progress on the scoring system. The updated plan involves a scoring calculation that activates upon quiz completion, displaying the result to the user. I've also integrated three CDNs (`pdfjs-dist`, `xlsx`, and `mammoth`) into the app using `esm.sh` to extract text from various file formats. This allows me to keep the heavy processing within the frontend and reduces potential API calls.


**Defining Interactive Quiz States**

I'm now diving into the core logic of the interactive quiz. I need a state to store `userAnswers`, track if the quiz `isSubmitted`, and the `score` calculation logic.  UI-wise, I'm updating the input section to include a file dropzone. `QuizDisplay` will evolve into `InteractiveQuiz` with appropriate inputs and a submit button. A `ScoreSummary` component will also be added with visual feedback for correct/incorrect answers.  Component-wise, I'll need `FileUpload`, `InteractiveQuiz`, and `ScoreSummary` components. I'll utilize `pdfjs-dist`, `mammoth`, and `xlsx` for file extraction and implement text-based prompting.


**Structuring Quiz Interactions**

I'm now refining the different quiz question types for the interactive component.  I've defined the UI for multiple choice, true/false (judgement), and fill-in-the-blank questions.  The fill-in-the-blank now has case-insensitive comparison and trimming. For short answer questions, I'm leaning toward providing an answer key for self-assessment, because automated grading proves too slow or complex. This leaves me with the first 27 questions being automatically graded. I am updating my files with the latest implementation of the grading logic and UI components.

entries.description: Add external libraries for file parsing (PDF, Word, Excel) via script tags.
New service to handle text extraction from various file formats.
Enhanced component for online quiz interaction and scoring.
Update App to support file uploads and switch to InteractiveQuiz component.

payload.text: 输入的学习资料修改为可以多份同时上传，在线答题后，给出的得分要以满分100来换算，简答题为10分一题，其他的70分按一定的规则进行分配

**Expanding File Uploads**

I've been thinking about the learning material upload feature. My main focus is on enabling multiple file uploads to enhance user experience. I'm also revising the scoring system for assessments. The new scale will be 100 points, with short answer questions contributing 30 points.


**Refining Assessment Scoring**

I'm now revising the assessment scoring system. I've broken down the 70 points for objective questions. Specifically, I'm focusing on the point allocation for Multiple Choice, Judgement, and Fill-in-the-Blank. Also, I'm adjusting my `App.tsx` file for multi-file upload capabilities and updating `components/InteractiveQuiz.tsx` to handle the new scoring logic, including the objective question weights.


**Revising Score Calculation**

I've been working on refining the final score calculation. Currently, I am updating the display to show the score out of 100. I need to make some key decisions. Should I provide a way for users to "self-check" the short answers, or simply calculate the objective score out of 70 and clarify the remaining 30 depends on the short answers? The `services/fileParser.ts` file already handles multiple files pretty well, but I need to ensure `App.tsx` loops through them correctly.


**Adjusting Scoring Display**

I'm now revising how the score is presented to the user. I've decided to display the objective score out of 70 and the short answer score out of 30. This avoids potential confusion with a complex weighted score. I will also be displaying how many files are uploaded. The updated UI includes the new score banner in the Interactive Quiz component. I'm focusing on simplicity and clarity for the user interface.

entries.description: Update App component to support multiple file uploads, aggregate text from all files, and manage a list of uploaded files.
Update scoring logic to 100-point scale: 30 points for Short Answers (10 each) and 70 points for Objective questions (redistributed).
